releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Fixes task checkbox behavior in list autocompletion and rendering.

- Continues task list items with unchecked `[ ]` syntax.
- Preserves existing checked state when rewriting an existing task item.
- Supports task modifiers for unordered, ordered, fancy, hash, and example lists.
- Renders interactive checkboxes in Live Preview and Reading Mode.
- Expands checkbox syntax back to editable source when keyboard navigation reaches it.

Addresses the checklist regression reported in #10.

- Added readable fenced-div shorthand for Live Preview, Reading mode, panels, and Pandoc export.
- Added fenced-div title metadata, opt-in `&` numbering, hierarchical placeholders, and `@id` cross-references.
- Added `lua_filter/FencedDivExtendedSyntax.lua` for readable shorthand, generated titles, cross-references, and portable export styling.
- Improved fenced-div panel coverage and release documentation.

## Validation

- `npm run lint`
- `npm test`
- `npm run build`
- `npm run test:e2e`

## 1.4.7 - Footnote Sidebar Panel

- Added a dedicated Footnotes sidebar tab that lists every footnote definition with rendered markdown and math support.
- Clicking the footnote index now focuses the editor immediately after the matching `[^label]` reference for quick editing.
- Added jump-to-definition support and highlight for footnote content entries.

releases.author: ErrorTzy
releases.description:
**Issue**: Footnote syntax like `[^1]` was incorrectly being matched as superscript, causing rendering issues
- **Fix**: Updated regex patterns to exclude square brackets from superscript/subscript matching
- **Impact**: Footnotes and superscripts now work correctly together without conflicts

releases.author: ErrorTzy
releases.description: - Fixed superscript/subscript patterns breaking math expressions with ^ or ~ symbols

releases.author: ErrorTzy
releases.description:
## What's New

### Features
- **Definition List Panel**: Added new Definition List panel to the List Panel View sidebar
  - Displays all definition lists from the current document in a two-column format (terms | definitions)
  - Full markdown rendering support for terms including bold, italic, math expressions, and cross-references
  - Smart truncation with configurable limits (100 chars for terms, 300 chars for definitions)
  - Click definitions to navigate to the source in the editor
  - Hover for full content preview with rendered formatting
  - Now configurable in Settings → Panel Order alongside other panels

This release focuses on significant architectural improvements and code quality enhancements while fixing several user-facing bugs.

releases.author: ErrorTzy
releases.description:
# Release Notes - Version 1.4.0

## 🎉 Major Feature: Custom Label Lists

This release introduces **Custom Label Lists**, a powerful new feature for creating structured documents with cross-referenceable labeled items. This is particularly useful for academic writing, technical documentation, and logical arguments.

### What are Custom Label Lists?

Custom label lists use the `{::LABEL}` syntax to create uniquely labeled list items that can be referenced throughout your document:

```markdown
{::P} All humans are mortal.
{::Q} Socrates is human.
{::R} Therefore, Socrates is mortal.

From {::P} and {::Q}, we conclude {::R}.
```

### Key Features

#### 1. 🏷️ Flexible Labeling System
- Use any combination of letters, numbers, underscores, and apostrophes
- Examples: `{::Theorem1}`, `{::Lemma_2'}`, `{::Step_A}`

#### 2. 🔢 Auto-numbering with Placeholders
- Use `(#placeholder)` syntax for automatic sequential numbering
- Placeholders with the same name get the same number
- Example:
  ```markdown
  {::P(#a)} First principle     → (P1) First principle
  {::P(#b)} Second principle    → (P2) Second principle
  {::Q(#a)} Related to first    → (Q1) Related to first
  ```

#### 3. 🎯 Intelligent Auto-completion
- **Enhanced Suggestions**: Type `{::` to see all available labels with preview text
- **Rendered Preview**: Placeholders shown with their assigned numbers in suggestions
- **Smart Matching**: Find labels by typing either raw form (`P(#a)`) or rendered form (`P1`)
- **Visual Hints**: Numbers have dotted underlines, placeholders shown in grey

#### 4. 📝 Three-level Display System
When working with placeholders, the plugin provides context-aware rendering:
- **Collapsed**: Shows processed form when cursor is elsewhere
- **Semi-expanded**: Shows both processed and raw forms when cursor is on the line
- **Fully expanded**: Shows editable raw form when cursor is inside the marker

#### 5. ⚡ Auto-completion Support
- Press Enter after a custom label to create a new `{::}` marker
- Cursor automatically positioned for label entry
- Smart indentation handling

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### List Handling
- Fixed example list auto-completion no longer deleting labeled markers
- Fixed handling of `(@)` as a valid list marker
- Fixed list indentation issues in live preview
- Fixed custom label placeholder numbering when inserting items

### Code Quality
- Fixed missing ListPatterns imports that caused plugin crashes
- Removed hardcoded inline styles per coding guidelines

## 🔧 Technical Improvements

### Major Refactoring
- **Architecture**: Renamed plugin from "PandocLists" to "PandocExtendedMarkdown" throughout codebase
- **Code Organization**: Major reorganization following established coding guidelines
- **Pattern Centralization**: All regex patterns now centralized in ListPatterns class
- **Error Handling**: Added comprehensive error boundaries to all public methods
- **Type Safety**: Moved all interfaces to dedicated type files

### Performance Optimizations
- Optimized document scanning for label detection
- Improved caching for placeholder numbering
- Reduced redundant processing in suggestion system

## 📚 Documentation

- Updated ARCHITECTURE.md with new component descriptions
- Enhanced README.md with comprehensive feature documentation
- Added JSDoc comments throughout the codebase
- Improved inline documentation for complex logic

## 🎨 UI/UX Improvements

### Definition Lists
- Added toggle command for definition underline styles
- Improved definition list rendering in both preview and reading modes

### Visual Enhancements
- Custom label placeholders now display with dotted underlines
- Improved visual hierarchy in suggestion dropdowns
- Better contrast for placeholder text in grey

## 🧪 Testing

- Added comprehensive test suite for custom label functionality
- Increased test coverage for edge cases
- Added tests for placeholder numbering logic
- Validated cross-reference resolution

## 📦 Compatibility

- Minimum Obsidian version: 1.4.0
- Fully compatible with existing Pandoc workflows
- Lua filter included for Pandoc conversion: `/lua_filter/CustomLabelList.lua`
- 
---

**Full Changelog**: [v1.3.2...v1.4.0](https://github.com/ErrorTzy/obsidian-pandoc-extended-markdown/compare/1.3.2...1.4.0)

**Installation**: Available through Obsidian's Community Plugins browser or manually from the [releases page](https://github.com/ErrorTzy/obsidian-pandoc-extended-markdown/releases/tag/1.4.0).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
# Release Notes - Version 1.3.2

## 🎯 Overview
This release focuses on major code refactoring and architectural improvements to enhance maintainability, performance, and future extensibility of the Pandoc Extended Markdown plugin.

## 🔄 What's Changed

### Refactoring & Architecture
- Restructured the codebase with clear separation of concerns
- Introduced `PluginStateManager` for centralized and efficient state handling
- Created specialized `ReadingModeParser` and `ReadingModeRenderer` classes
- Added `ProcessorConfig` for better configuration management

### Performance Improvements
- Optimized reading mode processing with a more efficient rendering pipeline
- Enhanced widget system for faster and more reliable decoration rendering
- Improved parsing logic for example lists and super/subscript text

### Code Quality
- Better TypeScript type safety with proper interfaces
- Clearer module boundaries for improved maintainability
- More testable codebase structure
- Enhanced code organization following best practices

releases.author: ErrorTzy
releases.description:
# 🎉 Version 1.3.0 - Major Expansion & Rebranding

This release marks a significant expansion of the plugin's capabilities, transforming it from a lists-only plugin to a comprehensive Pandoc extended markdown renderer.

## 🚀 Breaking Changes
- **Plugin Renamed**: "Pandoc Lists" → "Pandoc Extended Markdown"
- **Plugin ID Changed**: `pandoc-lists` → `pandoc-extended-markdown`
- **Repository Renamed**: `obsidian-pandoc-lists` → `obsidian-pandoc-extended-markdown`
- All internal class names updated (e.g., `PandocListsPlugin` → `PandocExtendedMarkdownPlugin`)

> ⚠️ **Migration Required**: You'll need to disable the old "Pandoc Lists" plugin and enable the new "Pandoc Extended Markdown" plugin in your Obsidian settings.

## ✨ New Features

### Superscripts and Subscripts
- **Superscript syntax**: `^text^` renders as superscript (e.g., `2^10^` → 2¹⁰)
- **Subscript syntax**: `~text~` renders as subscript (e.g., `H~2~O` → H₂O)
- **Escaped spaces**: Use backslash for spaces in super/subscripts (e.g., `P~a\ cat~`)
- Full support in all contexts: paragraphs, lists, definition lists
- Works in both live preview and reading modes
- Cursor-aware rendering preserves source text while editing

### Auto-Renumbering for Lists
- Automatically renumbers list items when inserting new items
- Maintains proper sequence for alphabetic lists (A, B, C...)
- Maintains proper sequence for roman numerals (i, ii, iii...)
- Respects indentation levels (only affects same-level items)
- Toggle via settings panel

### Enhanced Definition Lists
- Support for spaces before markers (e.g., `  ~ Definition`)
- Improved parsing for edge cases
- Better handling of inline formatting within definitions

## 🐛 Bug Fixes
- Fixed superscripts/subscripts not rendering in definition list content
- Fixed definition list content showing as "undefined" in reading mode
- Improved robustness of definition list parsing
- Fixed various edge cases in list continuation

## 📚 Documentation
- Updated README with comprehensive feature documentation
- Added examples for all new syntax features
- Clarified current scope and limitations
- Updated all repository references

## 🔧 Technical Improvements
- Added dedicated parser module for superscripts/subscripts
- Improved error handling with descriptive messages
- Better separation of concerns in parsing logic
- Enhanced type safety throughout codebase

## 📦 Installation
Due to the renaming, please:
1. Disable/remove the old "Pandoc Lists" plugin
2. Install the new "Pandoc Extended Markdown" plugin
3. Your settings will need to be reconfigured

## 🙏 Acknowledgments
This plugin continues to be developed with the assistance of Claude Code.

---

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ErrorTzy/obsidian-pandoc-extended-markdown/compare/1.2.0...1.3.0

releases.author: ErrorTzy
releases.description:
## 🎉 New Features

### List Autocompletion System
- **Smart Enter key handling**: Automatically continue any Pandoc list type with the next appropriate marker
- **Context-aware detection**: Intelligently distinguishes between alphabetic and Roman numeral lists
  - Special handling for 'I'/'i': Defaults to Roman numeral unless preceded by 'H'/'h' in sequence
- **Tab/Shift-Tab support**: Indent and dedent list items with proper marker preservation
- **Empty list behavior**: Press Enter on empty list items to remove markers or dedent

### Supported List Types for Autocompletion
- Hash lists (`#.`)
- Alphabetic lists (`A.`, `B.`, `a)`, `b)`)
- Roman numerals (`I.`, `II.`, `i)`, `ii)`)
- Example lists (`(@label)`) with smart cursor positioning
- Definition lists (`:` and `~` markers)

### Interactive Features
- **Clickable list markers**: Click any list marker to place cursor
- **Tooltip previews**: Hover over example list references to see content
- **Toggle definition bold style**: New command to switch between explicit and implicit bold formatting

## 🔧 Improvements

### Performance & Architecture
- Optimized regex patterns with caching for better performance
- Centralized constants and error handling
- Enhanced TypeScript definitions for Obsidian APIs
- Refactored code structure for better maintainability

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed non-editable list markers issue
- Improved widget equality checks for proper updates
- Better memory management with proper cleanup

## 📝 Notes

This release focuses heavily on improving the editing experience with comprehensive autocompletion support for all Pandoc list types. The smart detection system makes working with complex lists much more intuitive, especially when dealing with ambiguous markers like 'I' that could be either alphabetic or Roman numeral.

## Full Changelog

- Add complete list autocompletion system with Enter/Tab/Shift-Tab support
- Implement context-aware Roman vs alphabetic detection
- Add special handling for 'I'/'i' defaulting to Roman
- Add interactive list markers with click handlers
- Add tooltip functionality for example references
- Add toggle definition bold style command
- Optimize performance with cached regex patterns
- Improve error handling and type safety
- Refactor code architecture for maintainability

releases.author: ErrorTzy
releases.description:
## What's New

### ✨ Strict Pandoc Mode
- Added settings tab with "Strict pandoc mode" toggle
- When enabled, only lists conforming to Pandoc standards are rendered
- Lists must have proper empty lines before/after blocks
- Capital letters with periods require double spacing
- Invalid lists are displayed as plain text

### 📝 New Commands
- **Check pandoc formatting**: Scans the current document and reports formatting issues
- **Format document to pandoc standard**: Automatically formats lists to meet Pandoc standards

### 🔧 Enhanced Definition List Support
- Tab/4-space indented paragraphs after definitions are now properly rendered as part of the definition
- Fixed Obsidian's code block styling for indented definition content
- Definition terms can be directly followed by definition markers (no empty line required)
- Improved cursor visibility and positioning in indented content

### 🛠️ Technical Improvements
- Added settings management system
- Implemented block-level validation for better performance
- Improved CSS override strategy for definition content
- Better handling of edge cases in definition list parsing

## Installation

Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from the assets below and place them in your vault's `.obsidian/plugins/pandoc-lists/` folder.

## Full Changelog

https://github.com/ErrorTzy/obsidian-pandoc-lists/compare/1.0.0...1.1.0
